description: Comprehensive eval suite covering all skill capabilities: token lookup, component props, platform rules, do/don't guidance, Figma push, visual fetch, JSON routing, Arabic/RTL, cross-platform misuse, code generation, content guidelines, email rules, gap analysis, and MD vs JSON performance comparison.
categories: P: MD vs JSON Performance Comparison

evals.prompt: I need to find the nodeId, Figma link, and fallback URL for the App Button component. Where exactly do you look this up, and what is the nodeId?
evals.expected_output: Reads references/flow-app-v2.lookup.json, finds the 'buttons' entry, returns nodeId '5039:740', Figma link, and netlify fallback URL. Does NOT scan component-nodes.md first.
evals.passCondition: Uses references/flow-app-v2.lookup.json as the primary source. Correct nodeId returned.

evals.prompt: Where does the FDS skill look up which tokens a Web Input Field uses?
evals.expected_output: references/flow-web-v2.lookup.json → find 'input-field' entry → read tokens.background, tokens.border, tokens.text arrays. Falls back to references/design-tokens.md only if not in lookup JSON.
evals.passCondition: Names the correct JSON file and path. Does not say 'components-data.md' as the first answer.

evals.prompt: I want to know the canonical prop names for an FDS Accordion — where exactly do you find this and what are they?
evals.expected_output: Reads references/flow-app-v2.lookup.json or references/flow-web-v2.lookup.json accordion entry props.variants and props.booleans. Returns: Expanded (True/False), Language (English/Arabic), Show Icon, Show Badge, Title, Title (Ar).
evals.passCondition: Looks up lookup JSON first, not components-data.md first. Correct canonical prop names returned.

evals.prompt: What file do I read to find out if a component is allowed on App but not Web?
evals.expected_output: references/platform-rules.json — specifically the platforms.app.keyRules and platforms.web.forbiddenAppPatterns, or the platforms.web.keyRules listing web-only components.
evals.passCondition: Identifies references/platform-rules.json as the correct file, not SKILL.md inline text alone.

evals.prompt: I need the Figma file key for Flow Web V2. What file contains this and what is the key?
evals.expected_output: references/flow-web-v2.lookup.json → meta.fileKey = '6JoGJ2tuMzkJ0b5gnAX7UR'. Alternatively any lookup JSON meta.fileKey for the Web library.
evals.passCondition: Reads correct lookup JSON meta field. Returns the correct file key.

evals.prompt: What are all the valid Type values for the App Button component?
evals.expected_output: From references/flow-app-v2.lookup.json buttons entry: Primary, Secondary, White Outlined, CTA, Ghost, Destructive. All Title Case.
evals.passCondition: Reads lookup JSON. Returns correct values. All in Title Case.

evals.prompt: Someone sets a button's State to 'hover' on the App library. What is wrong with this?
evals.expected_output: Two violations: (1) 'hover' should be 'Hover' (Title Case required per prop-lookup.json). (2) App components do not have a Hover state — use Pressed instead. Hover is a Web/desktop-only concept.
evals.passCondition: Catches both the casing violation AND the platform-incorrect state. References prop-lookup.json for casing rule and platform-rules.json / lookup JSON for the App state list.

evals.prompt: What is the correct Arabic content prop pattern in FDS? Someone is using 'Arabic Title' — is that right?
evals.expected_output: 'Arabic Title' is wrong. The correct pattern is 'Title (Ar)' — the English prop name with ' (Ar)' suffix (space before parenthesis). Defined in prop-lookup.json synonyms: 'Arabic Title' → 'Title (Ar)'.
evals.passCondition: Identifies wrong pattern. Returns correct (Ar) suffix pattern. References prop-lookup.json.

evals.prompt: A developer set a boolean prop to 'on' instead of 'true'. Is this valid in FDS?
evals.expected_output: Invalid. FDS boolean props use True/False only — never On/Off, Yes/No, 1/0. This is defined in prop-lookup.json and platform-rules.json crossPlatformComponents.alignmentRules.
evals.passCondition: Rejects 'on'. States True/False rule. References prop-lookup.json.

evals.prompt: What sub-components does the App Action Footer have, and what are their nodeIds?
evals.expected_output: Reads references/flow-app-v2.lookup.json, finds action-footer entry (or notes it's not yet populated and falls back to references/component-nodes.md). nodeId 5616:2411, sub-components: Action Footer Summary (5616:2453) and .Options (5617:2200).
evals.passCondition: Attempts lookup JSON first. Returns correct nodeIds from whichever source has them.

evals.prompt: Does the Web Button have a Viewport prop? What are the valid values?
evals.expected_output: Yes — Web components always have Viewport. Valid values: Desktop, Tablet, Mobile, mWeb. This is a Web-specific required prop that App components do not have.
evals.passCondition: Confirms Viewport on Web Button. Lists all 4 correct values. Notes App doesn't have this prop.

evals.prompt: What is the default variant that should be applied when first inserting the App Alert component into a Figma design?
evals.expected_output: From references/flow-app-v2.lookup.json alert.figmaPush.defaultVariant: Type=Informational, State=Default, Language=English.
evals.passCondition: Reads figmaPush.defaultVariant from lookup JSON. Returns correct three-prop default.

evals.prompt: A designer wants to use the App Bottom Navigation Bar in a Web design file. Is this allowed?
evals.expected_output: Not allowed. App Bottom Navigation Bar is an App-only component. On Web, use Super Nav (top navigation) and/or Footer. This is listed in references/platform-rules.json platforms.web.forbiddenAppPatterns.
evals.passCondition: Rejects the cross-platform use. Names the correct Web alternatives. References platform-rules.json.

evals.prompt: Can I use the Web Modal component in an iOS app design?
evals.expected_output: No. Web Modal is Web-only. On App, use Modal Alert (for simple dialogs) or Drawer (for bottom-sheet panels). This is in platform-rules.json platforms.app.forbiddenWebPatterns.
evals.passCondition: Rejects Web Modal on App. Names Modal Alert and Drawer as App alternatives.

evals.prompt: Does the App platform need a Viewport prop on its components?
evals.expected_output: No. App components never have a Viewport prop — they are inherently mobile-sized. Viewport is Web-only (Desktop, Tablet, Mobile, mWeb). This is in platform-rules.json platforms.app.viewportProp: false.
evals.passCondition: Correctly states no Viewport on App. Explains it's Web-only.

evals.prompt: I'm building an email template. Can I use the Web Input Field component in it?
evals.expected_output: No. Email components are a completely separate library. Never use App or Web components in email. Email has its own 42-component library (flow-email.lookup.json). Additionally, email cannot use the same CSS/HTML patterns as Web components.
evals.passCondition: Rejects Web component in email. States email is a separate library. References flow-email.lookup.json.

evals.prompt: What is the difference between mWeb and the App Mobile platform?
evals.expected_output: mWeb is a browser viewport variant for Web components (responsive mobile web on qatarairways.com). App Mobile is a native iOS/Android screen. They are completely different: mWeb uses Web components at narrow viewport; App uses native App components. Never substitute one for the other.
evals.passCondition: Clearly distinguishes mWeb (browser) from App (native). Explains they cannot be interchanged.

evals.prompt: What happens to the Status Bar component when building a Web page?
evals.expected_output: The Status Bar is an App-only component — it is an iOS/Android OS hardware element. It never appears in Web designs. This is explicitly listed in platform-rules.json platforms.web.forbiddenAppPatterns.
evals.passCondition: States Status Bar is App-only (OS hardware). Correctly rejects its use on Web.

evals.prompt: What should I avoid when placing a Primary button on an App screen?
evals.expected_output: From references/flow-app-v2.lookup.json buttons.donts: (1) Do not place more than one Primary button in the same screen section. (2) Do not use Hover state — App uses Pressed. (3) Do not resize below 44px height (WCAG). (4) Do not hardcode colour — use tokens. (5) Do not use a button for list item navigation.
evals.passCondition: Reads lookup JSON donts array. Returns at least 3 of the correct don'ts for the App button.

evals.prompt: What must I always do when showing an Error Message in an App Input Field?
evals.expected_output: Always pair Show Error Message=True with State=Error simultaneously. Both must be set together — never show the error message indicator without also setting the Error state.
evals.passCondition: Returns the paired State=Error + Show Error Message=True rule from input-field.dos array.

evals.prompt: Can I nest accordions inside each other in FDS?
evals.expected_output: No — FDS accordion donts explicitly state: do not nest accordions more than one level deep. Source: references/flow-app-v2.lookup.json accordion.donts.
evals.passCondition: Returns the no-nesting rule from accordion donts.

evals.prompt: Is it acceptable to show an FDS Alert with only a Title and no Description?
evals.expected_output: No. Alert donts state: 'Do not omit Description when Title is present' and 'Do not write vague messages'. Alert dos state: 'Always include a Description explaining what happened and what to do next.' A Title-only alert is against FDS guidelines.
evals.passCondition: Correctly rejects Title-only alert. References the dos/donts from alert entry in lookup JSON.

evals.prompt: How many Primary buttons can I have in a single screen section on the App?
evals.expected_output: Exactly one. FDS button donts: 'Do not place more than one Primary button in the same screen section.' Multiple primary CTAs create visual hierarchy confusion.
evals.passCondition: Returns max one primary button rule. Sources it from buttons.donts in lookup JSON.

evals.prompt: Walk me through the correct steps to push an FDS App Button component to a Figma design file using MCP.
evals.expected_output: 9-step protocol from references/figma-push.json: (1) Look up nodeId from flow-app-v2.lookup.json, (2) get_screenshot to confirm visual, (3) platform check, (4) start-editing-transaction on TARGET file (not library), (5) perform-editing-operations to insert by nodeId, (6) set props from defaultVariant + override, (7) bind fills/spacing to Foundation variables, (8) validate checklist, (9) commit-editing-transaction or cancel on failure.
evals.passCondition: Reads references/figma-push.json. Returns correct MCP tool names. Notes transaction is on target file not library.

evals.prompt: What MCP tool do I call to push a design operation to Figma?
evals.expected_output: perform-editing-operations (within a transaction). The full sequence is: start-editing-transaction → perform-editing-operations → commit-editing-transaction. Source: references/figma-push.json mcpTools.
evals.passCondition: Names all three correct MCP tools in the right sequence.

evals.prompt: When pushing to Figma, can I use a hardcoded hex value for a button's background fill?
evals.expected_output: No. All fills must be bound to FDS Figma variables from the Foundation file (fileKey: 7D95UtZ3mvVWUvv1P3CGGW) — not hardcoded. Binding to FBrandColorBackgroundPrimary variable instead of #8E2157. Source: references/figma-push.json variableBindingRules.
evals.passCondition: Rejects hardcoded hex. Names Foundation file as source of variables. Explains variable binding.

evals.prompt: A Figma push transaction fails validation. What should I do?
evals.expected_output: Call cancel-editing-transaction immediately with the captured transaction ID. Log every failing check (component name + prop + violation). Fix all issues before starting a new transaction. Do NOT leave a stale transaction open. Source: references/figma-push.json errorRecovery and workflow step 9.
evals.passCondition: Names cancel-editing-transaction. States all failures must be logged before retrying.

evals.prompt: I want to push an App Accordion to a Figma file. What default variant props should it start with?
evals.expected_output: From references/flow-app-v2.lookup.json accordion.figmaPush.defaultVariant: Expanded=False, Language=English. Required props: Expanded. Variable scope: flow-app-v2.
evals.passCondition: Reads figmaPush.defaultVariant from accordion entry. Returns Expanded=False, Language=English.

evals.prompt: Can I detach a library component instance in Figma to override one of its variables?
evals.expected_output: No. Detaching is explicitly forbidden in figma-push.json variableBindingRules.forbidden. If customisation is needed beyond what the component's props allow, raise an FDS gap request to the design system team.
evals.passCondition: Rejects detaching. Names the FDS gap process as the correct path.

evals.prompt: What are the required props that must always be set when inserting a Web Button into Figma?
evals.expected_output: From references/flow-web-v2.lookup.json button.figmaPush.requiredProps: Type, State, Viewport. All three must always be set — Viewport is mandatory on all Web components.
evals.passCondition: Returns all three required props including Viewport. Sources from figmaPush.requiredProps.

evals.prompt: I want to see what the App Alert component looks like. What is the exact sequence to fetch its visual?
evals.expected_output: (1) Look up references/flow-app-v2.lookup.json alert entry → get figma.nodeId and meta.fileKey. (2) Call Figma MCP get_screenshot(nodeId='5662:1964', fileKey='1fefe3u3ntgI9SfbU2gppC'). (3) On failure → fetch figma.fallback netlify URL. (4) On failure → FDS MCP inspect_frame. (5) On all failures → proceed text-only from props/tokens, flag it.
evals.passCondition: Uses lookup JSON for nodeId/fileKey. Correct 5-step fallback chain. Never uses local cache.

evals.prompt: The Figma MCP get_screenshot call failed. What is the fallback URL for the App Accordion component?
evals.expected_output: From references/flow-app-v2.lookup.json accordion.figma.fallback: 'https://flow-ds-mcp.netlify.app/components/app/accordion.png'
evals.passCondition: Reads lookup JSON figma.fallback field. Returns correct netlify URL.

evals.prompt: What file should I check first to find the nodeId and screenshot fallback for a Web Super Nav?
evals.expected_output: references/flow-web-v2.lookup.json — find the 'super-nav' entry in components[], read figma.nodeId and figma.fallback. This is the primary source before checking component-nodes.md.
evals.passCondition: Names the correct Web lookup JSON as primary source. Does not default to component-nodes.md first.

evals.prompt: What are the exact CSS token names and hex values for all FDS warning state colors — text, background, border, and icon?
evals.expected_output: text-warning: #7A5E00; background-warning: (read from design-tokens.md); border-warning: #FCC500; icon-warning: #7A5E00. All four tokens exist as full sets.
evals.passCondition: Returns all four warning tokens. Correct hex values. Does NOT use #B68E00 for text-warning (common hallucination).

evals.prompt: Review this CSS: `.f-card { background: #F2F3FA; border: 1px solid #DBDEE8; border-radius: 8px; box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(31,33,43,0.08); }` — list every FDS violation.
evals.expected_output: 4 violations: (1) background #F2F3FA hardcoded — use var(--f-card-bg) → var(--f-brand-color-background-default). (2) border 1px hardcoded — use var(--f-brand-border-size-default). (3) border-color #DBDEE8 hardcoded — use var(--f-card-border-color) → var(--f-brand-color-border-default). (4) border-radius 8px hardcoded — use var(--f-brand-radius-base). (5) box-shadow literal value — use var(--f-brand-shadow-medium). (6) brand tokens used directly in rules — should go through component tokens.
evals.passCondition: Identifies all hardcoded values. Provides correct token replacements. Notes component token layer missing.

evals.prompt: Can I use --f-base-color-solid-burgundy-400 directly in a component rule?
evals.expected_output: No. Base tokens are raw primitives and must never be used in components. The correct chain is: base → brand → component. In a component you use the component token (e.g. --f-button-bg), which maps to the brand token (--f-brand-color-background-primary), which references the base token. Base tokens are code-internal only.
evals.passCondition: Rejects base token in component. Explains 3-tier chain. Gives correct alternative.

evals.prompt: What shadow token should I use for a sticky bottom action bar? What direction does the shadow go?
evals.expected_output: --f-brand-shadow-medium-n (upward shadow: 0 -2px 8px var(--f-brand-color-shadow-default)). The -n suffix means negative/upward, designed specifically for sticky bottom bars and bottom sheets.
evals.passCondition: Returns correct -n token. Explains upward direction. Does not use -medium or custom shadow.

evals.prompt: I wrote this focus rule: `.f-button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #8E2157; outline-offset: 2px; }` — what's wrong?
evals.expected_output: Three violations: (1) 2px hardcoded — use var(--f-brand-border-size-focused). (2) #8E2157 burgundy is wrong for focus — focus ring must be dark: var(--f-brand-color-border-focused) = #1F212B, not burgundy. (3) outline-offset 2px hardcoded — must use a spacing token: var(--f-brand-space-xs).
evals.passCondition: Catches all 3 violations including the wrong focus color. Burgundy = CTA buttons only, not focus rings.

evals.prompt: What spacing token do I use for 20px padding and 56px margin? Do brand aliases exist for these?
evals.expected_output: 20px = --f-base-space-5. 56px = --f-base-space-14. Neither has a brand alias — they are exceptions. Use the base tokens directly: --f-base-space-5 and --f-base-space-14. This is one of the known gaps in the brand alias coverage.
evals.passCondition: Correctly identifies these two have NO brand aliases. Returns the base token names. Does not invent fake brand aliases.

evals.prompt: What is the correct hex for --f-brand-color-background-muted? Is it a light grey or a dark grey?
evals.expected_output: #4A525D — dark grey. This is a common trap: 'muted' sounds light but maps to a dark grey in FDS. It is NOT #EEEEF0 or similar light values. Source: design-tokens.md Brand Color Tokens.
evals.passCondition: Returns #4A525D. Explicitly states it is dark grey, not light. Flags it as a common mistake.

evals.prompt: What icon prefix and size should I use for the bottom navigation bar tabs on the App?
evals.expected_output: ic_nav_menu_ prefix at 32px. These are specifically designed for the Bottom Navigation Bar tab icons. Do not use ic_nav_ (24px) for this context. Source: references/flow-icons-v4.lookup.json sizingRules and platform-rules.json platforms.app.iconSizes.
evals.passCondition: Returns ic_nav_menu_ at 32px. Distinguishes from standard ic_nav_ 24px icons.

evals.prompt: A designer needs an icon that doesn't exist in the FDS icon library. What is the correct protocol?
evals.expected_output: Icon gap protocol (from flow-icons-v4.lookup.json iconGapProtocol): (1) Flag: 'Icon gap: [description] — no FDS equivalent exists'. (2) Use the closest available FDS icon as a temporary placeholder. (3) Suggest a name following FDS convention (ic_nav_{name} or ic_badge_{name}). (4) Recommend raising with the FDS design system team. Never embed a custom SVG silently.
evals.passCondition: Returns all 4 steps of icon gap protocol. Forbids silent custom SVG use.

evals.prompt: Can I resize an ic_nav_ icon to 32px to make it visually bigger?
evals.expected_output: No. FDS uses exactly two icon sizes: 24px for ic_nav_ and 48px for ic_badge_. You must never set font-size to any other value. If you need more visual space around the icon, adjust the wrapper container — not the icon itself. Source: flow-icons-v4.lookup.json rule_twoSizesOnly.
evals.passCondition: Rejects resizing. States 24px and 48px as the only two valid sizes. Says adjust wrapper not icon.

evals.prompt: How do I display a Qatar Airways logo badge, a United Kingdom flag, and an Amex payment icon in HTML?
evals.expected_output: Qatar Airways: (fds-code-share.css). UK flag: (fds-country-flags.css). Amex: (fds-payment-badges.css). All three CSS files are self-contained with SVG data URIs.
evals.passCondition: Returns correct class patterns for all 3 icon types. Names the correct CSS files.

evals.prompt: How do I switch an App Button to Arabic layout in Figma? Is it by flipping the frame or using a prop?
evals.expected_output: Always use the Language=Arabic variant prop on the component. Never manually flip, mirror, or apply CSS transform: scaleX(-1). The Language prop triggers the correct RTL layout that the design system team has built into the component. Manual mirroring is forbidden per platform-rules.json strictGlobalRules.arabicRequiresVariant.
evals.passCondition: Instructs Language=Arabic prop. Explicitly forbids manual mirroring. References platform-rules.

evals.prompt: What font should I use for Arabic text in FDS?
evals.expected_output: Almarai (weight 300). For Farsi/Persian: Noto Sans Far. For standard Latin/English: Graphik Web. Jotia is NOT used for Arabic text. CSS: var(--f-base-type-family-arabic) resolves to Almarai, sans-serif.
evals.passCondition: Returns Almarai for Arabic. Notes Jotia is not for Arabic. Returns correct CSS variable.

evals.prompt: A component has a Title prop. What Arabic prop should be paired with it and how is it named?
evals.expected_output: Title (Ar) — with a space before the parenthesis. The pattern is {English prop name} (Ar). So Title → Title (Ar), Label → Label (Ar), Description → Description (Ar). Never 'Arabic Title', never 'Title_AR', never 'TitleAr'. Source: prop-lookup.json arabicRules.
evals.passCondition: Returns 'Title (Ar)' with space. Rejects all wrong patterns. References prop-lookup.json.

evals.prompt: Does every FDS component need a Language=Arabic variant?
evals.expected_output: No — only components that have structural RTL layout changes need a Language variant. 20 components in FDS use this prop. If only the text changes (no structural difference), a Language variant is not required. Adding dir=rtl in code handles text direction for components without a Language variant.
evals.passCondition: Clarifies that Language prop is for structural RTL changes only, not every component.

evals.prompt: A developer used the App Drawer component in a Web design instead of building a Sidesheet. What is wrong and what should they use?
evals.expected_output: Violation: App Drawer is App-only (opens from bottom). Web uses Sidesheet (opens from side — right or left). This cross-platform misuse is listed in platform-rules.json. Correct Web component: Sidesheet.
evals.passCondition: Identifies the violation. Explains functional difference (bottom vs side). Names Sidesheet as correct.

evals.prompt: A designer has placed an App Status Bar at the top of a Web homepage mockup. Identify the problem.
evals.expected_output: The Status Bar is an iOS/Android OS hardware element (shows carrier, time, battery). It must never appear in Web designs — it is App-only and represents an OS chrome element, not a UI component the Web team controls. Remove it from the Web design.
evals.passCondition: Identifies Status Bar as OS hardware element, App-only. Instructs removal from Web design.

evals.prompt: An App design is using the Web Calendar Picker component for a date selection flow. Is this correct?
evals.expected_output: No. Calendar Picker is Web-only. On App, date selection is handled through the Booking Widget sub-component 'Date Passenger Picker'. Source: platform-rules.json platforms.web.keyRules and App-only component list.
evals.passCondition: Rejects Web Calendar Picker on App. Names the correct App alternative.

evals.prompt: The team is using an Android Keyboard component in an iOS design. Is this a problem?
evals.expected_output: Yes. iOS Keyboard and Android Keyboard are separate components in the App library and must never be swapped. iOS designs must use the iOS Keyboard; Android designs must use the Android (Google) Keyboard. Source: platform-rules.json platforms.app.keyRules.
evals.passCondition: Identifies the OS keyboard mismatch. States they must not be interchanged.

evals.prompt: Build a complete FDS-compliant primary button and secondary button in HTML and CSS. Include the token map on the block, all interactive states (hover, focus-visible, disabled), and correct BEM class naming.
evals.expected_output: .f-button block with token map on the block selector. Rules consume only --f-button-* tokens. .f-button--secondary modifier only reassigns tokens. Hover: background. Focus-visible: dark outline (#1F212B), NOT burgundy. Disabled: disabled tokens, not opacity. Composite font shorthand. No hardcoded values.
evals.passCondition: Correct BEM. Token map on block. 3-tier chain respected. Focus ring is dark not burgundy. Disabled uses tokens not opacity.

evals.prompt: Show me a BEM modifier that changes an FDS button to a white outlined variant. What component tokens does it reassign?
evals.expected_output: .f-button--white-outlined { --f-button-bg: transparent; --f-button-bg-hover: var(--f-brand-color-background-dark-40a); --f-button-text: var(--f-brand-color-text-light); --f-button-border-color: var(--f-brand-color-text-light); }. Modifier ONLY reassigns component tokens — adds no new CSS properties.
evals.passCondition: Modifier has zero new CSS properties. Only reassigns existing component tokens. All values are brand tokens.

evals.prompt: How do I build an FDS text input field with default, focused, error, and disabled states? Show correct HTML, BEM, and all CSS tokens.
evals.expected_output: .f-input block with token map. State modifiers or :focus-visible/:disabled pseudo-classes reassigning component tokens. Error state: --f-brand-color-border-error for border. Focused: --f-brand-color-border-focused (dark, not burgundy) with 2px. Disabled: --f-brand-color-background-disabled + text-disabled + cursor not-allowed. No opacity for disabled.
evals.passCondition: 4 states correct. Focus border is dark not burgundy. Disabled uses tokens. BEM correct.

evals.prompt: Why should I never set font-size, font-weight, and font-family individually in an FDS component?
evals.expected_output: FDS uses composite font shorthand tokens (--f-brand-type-*) that encode size/weight/line-height/family in one token. Setting properties individually (1) breaks the composite chain, (2) makes future type scale changes require hunting down individual properties, (3) is a BEM layer violation (brand tokens must not appear in rules, only in component token maps), (4) risks mismatched combinations.
evals.passCondition: Explains composite shorthand. Mentions 4-step component pattern. Explains chain benefits.

evals.prompt: What is the mandatory CSS file layer order in an FDS project?
evals.expected_output: Layer 1: FDS Core Tokens (:root --f-brand-* + --f-base-* from tokens.css). Layer 2: Component Tokens (--f-{component}-* on block selector). Layer 3: Component Rules (structural CSS consuming --f-{component}-* only). Layer 4: Custom/project-specific (must still use --f-brand-* tokens). Never mix layers.
evals.passCondition: Returns all 4 layers in correct order. States brand tokens are forbidden in Layer 3 rules.

evals.prompt: Write three FDS-compliant error messages: a payment that failed, a passport photo that's too blurry, and a seat that was just taken.
evals.expected_output: All three: sentence case, active voice, specific about what happened, solution-focused (what to do next), empathetic but not over-apologetic. E.g. 'Your payment couldn't be processed. Please check your card details and try again.' Not: 'Payment error occurred.'
evals.passCondition: All in sentence case. All active voice. All specific with a solution. Tone is warm and helpful not robotic.

evals.prompt: Is 'CONFIRM BOOKING' a valid FDS button label?
evals.expected_output: No — two violations: (1) All caps is not FDS copy style; use Sentence case: 'Confirm booking'. (2) Check if active voice is the best framing — 'Confirm booking' is acceptable. The FDS rule is sentence case for all UI text including buttons.
evals.passCondition: Catches all-caps violation. Returns sentence case version. References FDS content guidelines.

evals.prompt: What are the loyalty tier names in FDS and what are their correct background color tokens?
evals.expected_output: Burgundy: --f-brand-color-background-loyalty-burgundy (#780044). Silver: --f-brand-color-background-loyalty-silver (#787878). Gold: --f-brand-color-background-loyalty-gold (#8C5617). Platinum: --f-brand-color-background-loyalty-platinum (#1A1A1A). Tier prop values match these names exactly.
evals.passCondition: Returns all 4 tiers. Correct token names. Correct hex values.

evals.prompt: I need a 'typing indicator' component (three animated dots showing AI is responding) — does this exist in FDS?
evals.expected_output: FDS gap: f-typing-indicator. This component does not exist in the FDS library. Build it using FDS naming (f-typing-indicator), FDS motion tokens (--f-brand-motion-duration-quick for dot animation), FDS color tokens, and BEM structure. Flag to design system team: 'FDS gap: f-typing-indicator — animated loading state for AI/conversational UI. Candidate for formalisation.'
evals.passCondition: Correctly identifies it as an FDS gap. Builds using FDS conventions. Flags with correct format. Does NOT invent a fake library component.

evals.prompt: Is there an FDS icon for an AI sparkle/magic wand effect?
evals.expected_output: Icon gap: no FDS icon currently exists for AI sparkle/magic. Use the closest available (e.g. ic_nav_star or ic_nav_help as temporary placeholder). Suggest: ic_nav_ai-sparkle. Recommend raising with FDS design team. Never embed a third-party sparkle SVG silently.
evals.passCondition: Identifies as icon gap. Suggests a placeholder from existing set. Proposes FDS-style name. Flags to team.

evals.prompt: I'm building a login screen for the App. What FDS components should I include and in what order?
evals.expected_output: From references/patterns.md login/signup pattern: Status Bar (top, iOS) → Navigation Bar (back button, 'Sign in' title) → Input Field (email, password with password variant) → Helper Text (optional) → App Button (Primary 'Sign in') → Link (forgot password) → Divider → Social Media Buttons (Apple, Google) → Bottom Navigation Bar (if persistent nav). All from App library.
evals.passCondition: All components from App library. Correct vertical order. Includes Status Bar. Uses patterns.md.

evals.prompt: What is a Build Manifest and when should one be generated?
evals.expected_output: A Build Manifest (build-manifest.md) is generated alongside code for every UI build. It is a human-readable plain-language document listing: (1) FDS library components used (name, platform, purpose). (2) New/custom components built on FDS foundations. (3) Icons used. (4) Icon gaps. The code stays pure FDS — the manifest is the human communication layer.
evals.passCondition: Explains purpose correctly. States it's generated alongside code always. Lists the 4 sections.

evals.prompt: Can I use CSS Grid for layout in an FDS email template?
evals.expected_output: No. Email templates must use table-based layout only — not CSS Grid or Flexbox. Email clients (especially Outlook) do not reliably support modern CSS layout models. Source: references/flow-email.lookup.json platformRestrictions.noCSSGrid and platform-rules.json platforms.email.keyRules.
evals.passCondition: Rejects CSS Grid for email. States table-based layout only. References email restrictions.

evals.prompt: Should I use Jotia font for headings in an FDS email template?
evals.expected_output: No. Jotia is NOT supported in most email clients. For email headings, use Graphik Bold weight with the email font stack: 'Graphik Web', Arial, sans-serif. This is specified in platform-rules.json platforms.email.keyRules and flow-email.lookup.json platformRestrictions.
evals.passCondition: Rejects Jotia for email. Returns correct Graphik Bold alternative with correct font stack.

evals.prompt: Can I use CSS custom properties (CSS variables) in an FDS email template?
evals.expected_output: No. CSS custom properties (--f-brand-*) are not supported by most email clients. In email, all CSS must be inlined with literal values — not token references. Source: flow-email.lookup.json platformRestrictions.noCSSVariables.
evals.passCondition: Rejects CSS variables in email. Explains inline CSS with literal values required.

evals.prompt: What is the maximum content width for an FDS email template?
evals.expected_output: 600px. This is the standard max-width for email templates (flow-email.lookup.json emailSpec.maxWidth: 600). Desktop email clients render at this width; mobile email clients stack to single column via media queries.
evals.passCondition: Returns 600px. Notes single-column mobile stacking.

evals.category: P: MD vs JSON Performance Comparison
evals.prompt: [JSON route] What are the valid State values for an App Button? Look it up in the JSON lookup file only.
evals.expected_output: From references/flow-app-v2.lookup.json → buttons.props.variants.State: Default, Pressed, Focused, Disabled, Loading, Success. No Hover state listed. Single file read, no markdown scanning.
evals.passCondition: Reads only references/flow-app-v2.lookup.json. Returns exact State array. Zero .md file reads.
evals.perfNote: Baseline JSON route — one file, indexed key access.

evals.category: P: MD vs JSON Performance Comparison
evals.prompt: [MD route] What are the valid State values for an App Button? Look it up in components-data.md only.
evals.expected_output: From references/components-data.md Buttons section: scans table rows to find State column values — Default, Pressed, Focused, Disabled, Loading, Success. Requires linear scan of multi-thousand-line markdown file.
evals.passCondition: Returns same correct values as P-01. Notes that this required scanning a large markdown file vs indexed JSON lookup.
evals.perfNote: Baseline MD route — linear scan, same data. Compare accuracy and tool-call overhead vs P-01.

evals.category: P: MD vs JSON Performance Comparison
evals.prompt: [JSON route] What is the nodeId and Figma fallback URL for the App Alert component?
evals.expected_output: From references/flow-app-v2.lookup.json → alert.figma.nodeId = '5662:1964', fallback = netlify URL in same entry. Single read, direct key access.
evals.passCondition: Reads lookup JSON only. Returns correct nodeId and fallback URL. Does not open component-nodes.md.

evals.category: P: MD vs JSON Performance Comparison
evals.prompt: [MD route] What is the nodeId for the App Alert component? Use only references/component-nodes.md.
evals.expected_output: Scans component-nodes.md App section to find Alert row, reads nodeId from the table. Returns the same nodeId value as P-03.
evals.passCondition: Returns same nodeId as P-03. Requires markdown table scan vs single JSON key access.
evals.perfNote: MD visual fetch route. Both should return the same nodeId — P-03 is faster due to indexed access.

evals.category: P: MD vs JSON Performance Comparison
evals.prompt: [JSON route] What do's and don'ts apply when using the App Bottom Navigation Bar?
evals.expected_output: From references/flow-app-v2.lookup.json → bottom-navigation-bar.dos[] and bottom-navigation-bar.donts[]. Returns structured list immediately — this data EXISTS ONLY in the JSON, not in any .md file.
evals.passCondition: Reads lookup JSON. Returns dos/donts arrays. Correctly notes this data has no .md equivalent.
evals.perfNote: JSON-exclusive data — no .md fallback exists. JSON wins by default.

evals.category: P: MD vs JSON Performance Comparison
evals.prompt: [MD route] What do's and don'ts apply when using the App Bottom Navigation Bar? Use only the .md files.
evals.expected_output: references/components-data.md does not contain structured do's/don'ts per component — only props, variants, and states. The skill cannot answer this question reliably from .md files alone. Must flag: 'Do/don't data is only in the JSON lookup file.'
evals.passCondition: Correctly acknowledges .md files lack do/don't data. Does not fabricate a list. Points to JSON as the correct source.
evals.perfNote: MD route fails on JSON-exclusive data. Confirms JSON is non-redundant for do/don't guidance.

evals.category: P: MD vs JSON Performance Comparison
evals.prompt: [JSON route] For the Figma push of a Web Modal, what is the defaultVariant and what variable scope does it use?
evals.expected_output: From references/flow-web-v2.lookup.json → modal.figmaPush.defaultVariant = {Type: 'Default', Viewport: 'Desktop', State: 'Default'}, modal.figmaPush.variableScope = 'Brand Color Tokens + Space Tokens'. Single file read.
evals.passCondition: Reads flow-web-v2.lookup.json. Returns correct defaultVariant and variableScope. No .md reads.
evals.perfNote: Figma push data is JSON-exclusive — no .md equivalent.

evals.category: P: MD vs JSON Performance Comparison
evals.prompt: [MD route] For the Figma push of a Web Modal, what is the defaultVariant? Use only .md files.
evals.expected_output: references/components-data.md contains component props and variants but NOT figmaPush instructions. The .md route cannot provide defaultVariant for Figma push. Must flag this gap and point to references/flow-web-v2.lookup.json.
evals.passCondition: Correctly identifies that figmaPush data is absent from .md files. Does not guess. Points to JSON.
evals.perfNote: MD route fails on figmaPush data. Another JSON-exclusive data category.

evals.category: P: MD vs JSON Performance Comparison
evals.prompt: [JSON route] What are the forbidden patterns for the Web platform?
evals.expected_output: From references/platform-rules.json → platforms.web.forbiddenPatterns[]. Returns 8 specific do-not patterns. Single file read, direct path access.
evals.passCondition: Reads platform-rules.json. Returns all 8 forbidden patterns. No SKILL.md inline scan needed.
evals.perfNote: platform-rules.json is the authoritative source; SKILL.md embeds a summary only.

evals.category: P: MD vs JSON Performance Comparison
evals.prompt: [MD route] What are the forbidden patterns for the Web platform? Use only SKILL.md.
evals.expected_output: SKILL.md Platform Guard Rules section contains a condensed version of the key rules but is NOT the authoritative list. It references platform-rules.json for full rules. Returns partial list — may miss edge cases present in the JSON.
evals.passCondition: Returns some correct forbidden patterns. Notes SKILL.md is summary-only and platform-rules.json is authoritative. Accuracy lower than P-09.
evals.perfNote: SKILL.md inline text is lossy summary. JSON is complete and machine-readable.

evals.category: P: MD vs JSON Performance Comparison
evals.prompt: [Fallback test] A designer asks for the props and variants of the FDS Web Carousel component. The JSON lookup file does not yet have this component populated. What do you do?
evals.expected_output: JSON lookup file checked first (references/flow-web-v2.lookup.json) — Carousel entry not found or empty. Falls back to references/components-data.md → find Web Carousel section → read props/variants from markdown table. Flags: 'Carousel not yet in lookup JSON — data sourced from components-data.md. Recommend exporting from dataset to populate JSON.'
evals.passCondition: Tries JSON first. Falls back to .md gracefully. Flags the gap. Does not fail silently.
evals.perfNote: Critical fallback path — .md files are STILL NEEDED for the 52 App + 84 Web components not yet in JSON.

evals.category: P: MD vs JSON Performance Comparison
evals.prompt: [Fallback test] A developer asks for the hex value of --f-brand-color-text-subtle. The lookup JSONs don't store hex values. What is the correct data source?
evals.expected_output: Hex values are not in lookup JSONs (they store token names, not raw values). Correct source: references/design-tokens.md or the colour token quick-reference in SKILL.md itself. Returns #6E7780. Notes the common wrong value #6B7280.
evals.passCondition: Correctly identifies that hex values live in design-tokens.md / SKILL.md, not lookup JSONs. Returns #6E7780. Does not look in flow-web-v2.lookup.json for this.
evals.perfNote: .md files remain the authoritative source for token hex values — NOT redundant yet.

evals.category: P: MD vs JSON Performance Comparison
evals.prompt: [Redundancy audit] Which .md reference files are fully covered by the new JSON files and which are still needed?
evals.expected_output: FULLY COVERED by JSON (candidates for deprecation once JSON is complete): component-nodes.md (superseded by figma.nodeId in each lookup JSON entry). PARTIALLY COVERED — still needed: components-data.md (needed for ~130 components not yet in lookup JSON), design-tokens.md (hex values + base-tier tokens not in lookup JSON), foundations.md (typography, spacing scale, grid — no JSON equivalent yet). NOT COVERED by JSON at all: content-guidelines.md, email-components.md, patterns.md — no replacement planned.
evals.passCondition: Correctly categorises component-nodes.md as nearest to redundant. Correctly flags that components-data.md is still needed as fallback. Does not mark design-tokens.md or foundations.md as redundant.
evals.perfNote: This is the MD redundancy audit. Only component-nodes.md can be deprecated after lookup JSONs are fully populated.

evals.category: P: MD vs JSON Performance Comparison
evals.prompt: [Routing discipline test] A developer asks: 'What is the subComponents list for the App Input Field?' Without being told which file to use, which source does the skill consult first?
evals.expected_output: Skill reads references/flow-app-v2.lookup.json first (JSON routing rule from SKILL.md pre-flight). Finds input-field.subComponents[]. Returns Label, Text Input, Helper Text, Icon (leading/trailing) with their nodeIds. Does NOT open components-data.md first.
evals.passCondition: Opens lookup JSON without being prompted. Returns correct subComponents list. Zero .md reads before JSON is checked.
evals.perfNote: Tests autonomous JSON-first routing discipline without explicit instruction.

evals.category: P: MD vs JSON Performance Comparison
evals.prompt: [Accuracy parity test] Compare: What token does the App Button primary background use — answer from JSON, then cross-check against design-tokens.md. Do the values agree?
evals.expected_output: JSON: references/flow-app-v2.lookup.json → buttons.tokens.background = '--f-brand-color-background-primary'. design-tokens.md: --f-brand-color-background-primary = #8E2157 (brand-tier, maps to base burgundy-400). Both sources agree on the token name. design-tokens.md adds the hex value the JSON does not store. Conclusion: JSON for token name, .md for hex value — complementary, not redundant.
evals.passCondition: Reads both sources. Both return same token name. Notes JSON gives semantic name, .md gives hex. Concludes they are complementary.
evals.perfNote: Parity test confirms JSON and .md are both correct but serve different query types.

evals.prompt: Build me a complete FDS QSuite tag component — correct color token, BEM structure, appropriate icon, and all display states.
evals.expected_output: --f-brand-color-tags-qsuite token used (read from design-tokens.md). .f-tag BEM block with .f-tag--qsuite modifier. Token map on block. ic_badge_qsuite icon at 48px for decorative use or ic_nav_ 24px for inline. All values through component tokens, no hardcoded hex.
evals.passCondition: Correct QSuite color token. BEM compliant. Tokens not hardcoded. Correct icon prefix and size.

evals.prompt: What is --f-brand-color-background-default? What hex value is it and what is a common wrong version?
evals.expected_output: #F2F3FA. Common wrong versions: 'subtle' (#F7F7F8) — that token does not exist in FDS. The correct token is --f-brand-color-background-default, NOT --f-brand-color-background-subtle.
evals.passCondition: Returns #F2F3FA. Names the -subtle trap. Returns correct token name.

evals.prompt: What does a _schemaDefinition in a lookup JSON file describe?
evals.expected_output: It is a documentation object inside the lookup JSON that defines the expected shape for each entry in the components[] array. It tells the figma-dataset-exporter tool what fields to populate (id, name, platform, figma, subComponents, props, tokens, dos, donts, figmaPush). Hand-authored fields (dos, donts, figmaPush) are preserved across re-exports.
evals.passCondition: Explains it as schema documentation for the exporter. Mentions hand-authored fields that persist.

evals.prompt: When pushing an App Accordion with Language=Arabic, is it acceptable to manually flip the component frame in Figma?
evals.expected_output: No. Manual flipping or mirroring is explicitly forbidden in figma-push.json componentInsertionRules.arabicComponents and platform-rules.json strictGlobalRules.arabicRequiresVariant. Always set the Language=Arabic prop on the library instance — the component already has the correct RTL layout built in by the design system team.
evals.passCondition: Rejects manual flip. Returns Language=Arabic prop instruction. References both JSON files.
